LLM Fundamentals.front: What is a token?
LLM Fundamentals.back: A chunk of text (~3/4 of a word) turned into a number the model works on

LLM Fundamentals.front: What is the 'context window'?
LLM Fundamentals.back: How many tokens the model can hold at once

LLM Fundamentals.front: In attention, what does the QUERY represent?
LLM Fundamentals.back: What a word is looking for

LLM Fundamentals.front: In attention, what does the KEY represent?
LLM Fundamentals.back: What a word advertises about itself

LLM Fundamentals.front: In attention, what does the VALUE represent?
LLM Fundamentals.back: The information a word hands over if attended to

LLM Fundamentals.front: In one phrase, what does attention do?
LLM Fundamentals.back: Every word checks every other word for relevance

LLM Fundamentals.front: Running several attention passes in parallel is called?
Why are context windows expensive/limited?
LLM Fundamentals.back: Work grows with the square of the sequence length

LLM Fundamentals.front: A decoder-only transformer generates text how?
Training stage 1: learning patterns from huge text is called?
What game does a model play during pretraining?
Training stage that teaches instruction-following?
Training stage that aligns to human preferences?
What does 'inference' mean?
LLM Fundamentals.back: Running the finished (frozen) model to generate text

LLM Fundamentals.front: Inference phase that processes the whole prompt at once?
Inference phase that writes output one token at a time?
What is quantization?
LLM Fundamentals.back: Storing weights in fewer bits to shrink the model

LLM Fundamentals.front: Quantization's main tradeoff?
GGUF is a [what]?
What does the KV cache store?
LLM Fundamentals.back: Keys and Values of previous tokens during decode

LLM Fundamentals.front: Why does long context get heavy at runtime?
LLM Fundamentals.back: The KV cache grows with length and becomes the bottleneck

LLM Fundamentals.front: Batching improves what?
Training a small model to mimic a big one is called?
Small model drafts tokens, big model verifies, this is?
Which layer is Jeff's hands-on LLM experience?
